title: Volume Holds Steady as Transaction Mix Shifts Upmarket
description: LI.FI processed $2.79B in volume over the last 30 days, up 2.9% versus the prior period, even as transaction count fell 23.9%. The divergence signals a shift toward larger average ticket sizes — fewer, higher-value flows — suggesting growing institutional and power-user activity on the protocol.
dataPoints: Implied avg tx size up ~35% MoM

title: LI.FI Revenue Up 24% — Outpacing Volume Growth 8x
description: Protocol revenue reached $279.7K in the last 30 days, a 24.2% jump that dramatically outpaces the 2.9% volume growth. Combined with $1.39M in integrator fees, this points to improving monetization per dollar routed and a healthier take-rate as the integrator economy matures.

title: 8M+ Wallets Now Routed Through LI.FI
description: The protocol surpassed 8.05M unique wallets all-time, with 165K net new wallets onboarded in the last 30 days alone. With a median of 2 transactions per wallet, LI.FI is functioning as core cross-chain infrastructure for a broad long-tail of users — not just a power-user tool.

title: Cross-Chain Captures 41% of Volume on 24% of Transactions
description: Same-chain swaps drove $1.64B across 2.0M transactions, while cross-chain delivered $1.15B in just 620K transactions. Cross-chain flows are roughly 6x larger per transaction on average — confirming bridging remains the high-value lane while same-chain swap routing handles everyday user activity.

title: Binance Wallet Emerges as Largest Distribution Partner
description: Binance Wallet leads integrator activity with 545K transactions across 16 chains, demonstrating LI.FI's role as the routing layer behind major exchange wallets. Combined with base-app (97K tx, 7 chains) and Crypto.com DeFi (12K tx, 19 chains), exchange-affiliated wallets are now the largest demand channel.

title: 67-Chain Coverage Cements Routing Layer Position
description: LI.FI now actively routes across 67 unique chains as both source and destination, with Ethereum-Arbitrum the dominant corridor at $157M. Newer entrants like Plasma, Monad, Katana, and HyperEVM appear in integrator chain lists, showing the protocol is integrating new ecosystems as they launch.
dataPoints: Emerging chains: Plasma, Monad, Katana, HyperEVM

title: 18.9% of Trades Settle With Positive Slippage
description: Of 1.82M transactions analyzed, 344.8K (18.9%) executed at better-than-quoted prices. This positive slippage rate is a meaningful execution-quality signal: nearly 1 in 5 routes returns more value than promised at quote time, validating the multi-solver routing approach across DEXs and bridges.
dataPoints: 10+ active routing tools in the mix

title: API Channel Now Drives 58% of Volume Across 1,018 Integrators
description: The LI.FI API delivered $1.61B across 1,018 distinct integrators, while Jumper (the consumer front-end) handled $1.18B from just 9 integrators. The split shows LI.FI has successfully transitioned from an app-led to an infrastructure-led business — with the API's long tail now the larger and more diversified channel.
